actionable_feedback: Add a plain-text export of all key workbook formulas and cell ranges (e.g., a Formulas_Export sheet or a CSV) containing exact formula strings for Record_Assessment, Priority calculation, Sensitivity scenarios, Remediation combination checks, and Decision logic.

In the memo, include 3-5 explicit example cell-coordinate tracebacks mapping final decision rows back to the embedded packet (e.g., Record_Assessment!A5 -> Packet_Embedded!R2). This removes ambiguity about row-level citations and traceability.
If any named ranges or programmatic constructs are used, also include their resolved cell ranges and definitions in the formulas export so graders without openpyxl can parse logic.

Provide a small tab or CSV with flattened Record_Assessment results (one row per packet record) showing computed metric values, priority score, assigned action class, and sensitivity scenario outcomes so numeric checks are reproducible without opening formulas.

Run and attach a brief deterministic check summary in the memo (e.g., a Formulas_Export verification block) that lists the selected remediation combo, cost calculation, and budget check with the exact cell references used to compute them.
evidence_refs: trace (trace summary and steps in Trace summary JSON)
feedback_summary: Baseline deliverables meet the rubric requirements: a formula-backed workbook, clear assumptions, sensitivity checks, and a documented final recommendation (targeted_mitigation). The primary residual risk is that the grader could not programmatically inspect workbook formulas (openpyxl unavailable), so there remains small uncertainty about exact cell-level formula strings and explicit row-to-packet cell citations.

workbook_trace_indicates: OOXML workbook created with sheets and formulas

recommended_new_artifacts: Record_Assessment_flat.csv (computed metrics and classes per record)
Updated validation_note.md with example cell-coordinate tracebacks
extra_model_fields.verifier_issues_summary: No hidden-reference leakage. Single material limitation: grader could not parse .xlsx programmatically (openpyxl unavailable) and relied on memo preview and trace. Recommended remedy: include plain-text formulas export and example cell-coordinate tracebacks.
